frozen_components: entity embeddings (pre-trained by TransE on Wikidata)
architecture.summary: ERNIE is a pre-trained language model that extends BERT by incorporating knowledge graph (KG) information. It consists of a textual encoder (T-Encoder, identical to BERT's Transformer) that processes token sequences, followed by a knowledgeable encoder (K-Encoder) comprising stacked aggregators that fuse token embeddings with entity embeddings from Wikidata (pre-encoded via TransE). Entity mentions in text are aligned to KG entities, and their fixed embeddings flow through multi-head self-attention and information fusion layers that combine token and entity information in a shared representation space.

biblio.title: ERNIE: Enhanced Language Representation with Informative Entities

claims.statement: ERNIE achieves significant improvements on entity typing, with strict accuracy of 57.19% on FIGER
ERNIE achieves F1 of 75.56% on Open Entity, improving precision by 2.05 and recall by 1.94 over BERT
ERNIE achieves F1 of 88.32% on FewRel, improving by 3.43 points over BERT's 84.89%
ERNIE achieves F1 of 67.97% on TACRED, improving by 1.97 points over BERT's 66.00%
ERNIE achieves comparable results with BERT on GLUE benchmark, performing consistently on large datasets (MNLI, QQP, QNLI, SST-2) with more variable performance on small datasets
In ablation on FewRel, dEA alone (without entity sequence input) increases F1 by 0.9 points over BERT to 85.79%
In ablation on FewRel, entity sequence input without dEA increases F1 by 0.9 points over BERT to 85.79%
The total parameters of ERNIE are about 340M, with the knowledgeable module accounting for approximately 40M parameters, having little impact on runtime performance
